2606.30398.title: ENC-ODE: Event-level Neurodegenerative Modeling in Continuous Time with Neural ODEs
2606.30398.paper_summary: Accurately predicting the temporal evolution of clinical biomarkers is crucial for the early diagnosis and management of neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's disease. However, this relies on longitudinal data to capture biomarker changes over time, which is often sparse and irregular due to the high cost, labor-intensive nature, and patient burden. To address these challenges, we propose ENC-ODE, an Event-level Neurodegenerative modeling in Continuous time with neural Ordinary Differential Equations. ENC-ODE predicts future biomarker evolution by modeling clinical events through diagnosis-conditioned continuous dynamics. A target-conditioned attention mechanism weights and aggregates event-level predictions for the target time and modality without history compression. Extensive experiments on Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) dataset demonstrate that ENC-ODE outperforms representative sequence models while offering a scalable and neuroscientifically grounded solution for clinical support. The code is available at https://github.com/JardinDelSol/enc-ode.
2606.30398.paper_summary_zh: 準確預測臨床生物標記的時間演變對於早期診斷和管理神經退行性疾病，例如阿茲海默症，至關重要。<lb/>然而，這依賴於縱向數據，以捕捉生物標記隨時間的變化，而這通常因高成本、勞動密集性和患者負擔而變得稀疏且不規則。<lb/>為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了ENC-ODE，一種基於神經常微分方程的事件級神經退行性建模方法，具有連續時間特性。<lb/>ENC-ODE通過基於診斷的連續動力學對臨床事件進行建模，預測未來的生物標記演變。<lb/>一種目標條件注意機制對目標時間和模態的事件級預測進行加權和聚合，而不進行歷史壓縮。<lb/>在阿茲海默症神經影像倡議（ADNI）數據集上的廣泛實驗表明，ENC-ODE在性能上超越了代表性的序列模型，同時為臨床支持提供了一種可擴展且基於神經科學的解決方案。<lb/>代碼可在 https://github.com/JardinDelSol/enc-ode 獲得。
2606.30398.author: Yujee Song et.al.
2606.30398.authors: Yujee Song, Seunghun Baek, Guorong Wu, Won Hwa Kim
